name: Ceffyl Dwr
description: The Ceffyl Dwr, meaning 'water horse' in Welsh, is a mythical creature of Welsh folklore. It is a malevolent water spirit that often appears as a beautiful horse, enticing unsuspecting travelers to mount it. Once mounted, the horse would bolt towards the nearest body of water – a lake, river, or the sea – and plunge into the depths, drowning its rider. Some tales say the Ceffyl Dwr would then vanish, leaving no trace of itself or its victim. Other stories claim the rider would be devoured. The Ceffyl Dwr is a dangerous and deceptive creature, a warning against trusting appearances and venturing too close to treacherous waters. Its origins likely lie in ancient Celtic beliefs about the power and danger of water spirits and the need to respect the natural world. It is a shape-shifting entity, able to adapt its appearance to lure prey, but it always retains an underlying aura of unnatural beauty. The Ceffyl Dwr embodies the unpredictable and destructive forces of nature, a reminder of the perils hidden beneath a serene surface. It serves as a cautionary tale against greed and the allure of the unknown, highlighting the importance of caution and respect when navigating the natural world.
subtitle: The treacherous Welsh water horse
appearance: The Ceffyl Dwr typically appears as a magnificent horse, often white or grey, with a flowing mane and tail. Its eyes are often described as unusually large and intelligent, but with a hint of wildness. Some accounts mention webbed feet or an abnormally long tail. Its skin might appear unnaturally smooth or shimmer slightly, betraying its aquatic nature. It often exudes an aura of unnatural beauty and power, making it irresistible to those who encounter it. However, a keen observer might notice subtle inconsistencies, such as seaweed tangled in its mane or a faint smell of salt water.
habitat: The Ceffyl Dwr inhabits lakes, rivers, streams, and even the sea in Wales. It prefers secluded areas and deep waters, often lurking near the edges of forests or in marshy landscapes. It is most commonly found in areas with strong currents or treacherous undercurrents, reflecting its nature as a dangerous and unpredictable force.
behavior: The Ceffyl Dwr is a cunning and predatory creature. It uses its beautiful appearance to lure unsuspecting travelers, particularly those who are lost, tired, or greedy. It feigns friendliness and offers a ride, only to betray its rider once they are far from safety. It is patient and calculating, waiting for the perfect opportunity to strike. Once it has claimed its victim, it shows no remorse, disappearing back into the water to await its next prey. It is a solitary creature, rarely seen in the company of others of its kind.

keyMyths.title: The Drowning of Llyn y Fan Fach
keyMyths.description: A traveler was lured onto the back of a Ceffyl Dwr and dragged into Llyn y Fan Fach, drowning. The lake is said to be haunted by his spirit.

sources.author: John Rhys
